units.content: function deterministic_hash produces canonical hex digest for Python objects
function handles dicts with sorted keys, lists, sets (sorted), dataclasses, and nested structures
function is used by memoized_detector cache key, seeded_detector input hash, and test harness
_canonicalize function converts obj to JSON-serializable canonical form
function handles None, bool, int, float, str, bytes, list, tuple, set, frozenset, dict, dataclass instances, and objects with __dict__
NaN floats need special representation as ['float', 'nan']
infinite floats need special representation distinguishing positive and negative infinity
sets and frozensets are sorted by their JSON-serialized canonical form before canonicalization
dict items are sorted by JSON-serialized canonical key form
deterministic ordering is necessary to ensure same input always produces same hash
dataclass instances are detected and their fields extracted
arbitrary objects with __dict__ are handled as fallback
repr() is used as final fallback for objects without __dict__
